attempts.error:
Predicate argument has wrong type:
  atLocation microwave countertop: microwave is not a agent
attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Warm up a piece of apple.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains a kitchen environment with a table and a countertop area featuring a microwave. The robot's task is to warm up a piece of apple, which requires identifying the relevant objects and their states.

**Objects and Their Types:**
- **Receptacles:** There is one microwave (a dark red appliance with a digital display showing "OFF" and a keypad) located on a countertop to the right of the table. It is an openable receptacle. There is also a black square pan on the table, which can be considered a receptacle for holding objects.
- **Objects:** There are three golden-yellow apples on the table, one red tomato, a loaf of brown bread, two salt/pepper shakers (glass with copper tops), a metal spoon, and a black pan. One of the salt/pepper shakers is on the countertop next to the microwave, while the other is on the table. The microwave is a receptacle and can heat objects.

**Quantities:**
- Apples: 3
- Salt/Pepper Shakers: 2
- Microwave: 1
- Pan: 1
- Spoon: 1
- Tomato: 1
- Bread: 1

**Spatial Relationships:**
- The three apples are on the table. One apple is near the center of the table, another is to the right near the tomato, and the third is to the left near the bread.
- The microwave is on the countertop to the right of the table. One salt/pepper shaker is placed on the countertop beside the microwave.
- The black pan is on the left side of the table.
- The spoon is on the table near the bread.
- The tomato is on the right side of the table, near one of the apples.
- The bread is in the center of the table.

**Current State of Objects Relevant to the Task:**
- The microwave is currently off (display reads "OFF") and closed. It is not opened.
- None of the apples are currently held by the agent or placed in any receptacle.
- None of the apples are sliced, heated, cooled, or cleaned.
- The robot is not currently at any object’s location.

**Visual Properties:**
- The apples are golden-yellow and round.
- The tomato is red and round with a green stem.
- The bread is oval-shaped and brown.
- The microwave is dark red with a glass door and a digital display.
- The pan is black and square with a handle.
- The salt/pepper shakers are transparent glass with copper-colored tops.
- The spoon is metallic and lies flat on the table.

To warm up an apple, the robot must first go to an apple, pick it up, go to the microwave, open it, place the apple inside, close the microwave, and then toggle it on to heat the apple. The microwave must be opened before placing the apple inside. The apple must be held by the robot before being placed in the microwave. The microwave is currently off and closed, so it needs to be toggled on after placing the apple inside.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem warm_apple)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        microwave - microwave
        apple1 apple2 apple3 - object
        pan - receptacle
        salt_shaker1 salt_shaker2 - object
        tomato - object
        bread - object
        spoon - object
        table - object
        countertop - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot table)
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
        (not (isHot apple1))
        (not (isHot apple2))
        (not (isHot apple3))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (holds robot apple1))
        (not (holds robot apple2))
        (not (holds robot apple3))
        (inReceptacle apple1 table)
        (inReceptacle apple2 table)
        (inReceptacle apple3 table)
        (inReceptacle tomato table)
        (inReceptacle bread table)
        (inReceptacle spoon table)
        (inReceptacle pan table)
        (inReceptacle salt_shaker1 table)
        (inReceptacle salt_shaker2 countertop)
        (atLocation microwave countertop)
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isHot apple1)
        (inReceptacle apple1 microwave)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem warm_apple)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        microwave - microwave
        apple1 apple2 apple3 - object
        pan - receptacle
        salt_shaker1 salt_shaker2 - object
        tomato - object
        bread - object
        spoon - object
        table - object
        countertop - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot table)
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
        (not (isHot apple1))
        (not (isHot apple2))
        (not (isHot apple3))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (holds robot apple1))
        (not (holds robot apple2))
        (not (holds robot apple3))
        (inReceptacle apple1 table)
        (inReceptacle apple2 table)
        (inReceptacle apple3 table)
        (inReceptacle tomato table)
        (inReceptacle bread table)
        (inReceptacle spoon table)
        (inReceptacle pan table)
        (inReceptacle salt_shaker1 table)
        (inReceptacle salt_shaker2 countertop)
        (atLocation microwave countertop)
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isHot apple1)
        (inReceptacle apple1 microwave)
    ))
)

    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Warm up a piece of apple.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene depicts a kitchen environment with a table and a microwave. The table holds several objects, and the microwave is situated on a countertop to the right.

**Objects and Types:**
- **Receptacles**: 
    - A black square pan with a handle, located on the left side of the table.
    - A red microwave oven with a digital display and keypad, positioned on a brown countertop to the right of the table. The microwave is an openable receptacle.
- **Objects**:
    - Three golden apples: one is placed near the center of the table, another is to the upper right, and the third is slightly below the upper right apple.
    - One red tomato, located to the right of the central apple.
    - One loaf of bread, situated in the center of the table.
    - Two salt/pepper shakers with copper-colored tops and glass bodies: one is in front of the pan, and the other is near the upper right corner of the table.
    - One metal spoon, placed near the upper right corner of the table, next to the apples.
    - A knife is not visible in the scene, so it is not present for slicing.

**Quantities:**
- 1 pan (receptacle)
- 1 microwave (receptacle)
- 3 apples (objects)
- 1 tomato (object)
- 1 loaf of bread (object)
- 2 salt/pepper shakers (objects)
- 1 spoon (object)

**Spatial Relationships:**
- The pan is on the left side of the table, with one salt/pepper shaker in front of it.
- The loaf of bread is in the center of the table.
- The three apples are clustered on the right side of the table: one near the center, one upper right, and one slightly below the upper right.
- The tomato is to the right of the central apple.
- The second salt/pepper shaker and the spoon are near the upper right corner of the table.
- The microwave is on a countertop to the right of the table, separate from the table surface.

**Current State of Objects Relevant to the Task:**
- The microwave is currently off, as indicated by the "OFF" display.
- None of the apples are currently in any receptacle or being held.
- No objects are sliced, cleaned, heated, or cooled.
- The pan is empty and not being used.
- The microwave is closed (not opened).

**Visual Properties:**
- The apples are golden-yellow and round.
- The tomato is bright red with a green stem.
- The bread is brown and oval-shaped.
- The pan is black with a long handle.
- The salt/pepper shakers have transparent glass bodies and shiny copper-colored tops.
- The spoon is metallic and silver.
- The microwave is dark red with a black control panel and a glass door.
- The table is beige, and the floor is tiled in a light brown color.

The robot must first navigate to an apple, pick it up, then proceed to the microwave, open it, place the apple inside, close the microwave, and toggle it on to heat the apple. The current state requires all these actions to be performed sequentially.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem warm_apple)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        microwave - microwave
        pan - receptacle
        apple1 apple2 apple3 - object
        tomato - object
        bread - object
        shaker1 shaker2 - object
        spoon - object
        table - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot table)
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isHot apple1))
        (not (isHot apple2))
        (not (isHot apple3))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (holds robot apple1))
        (not (holds robot apple2))
        (not (holds robot apple3))
        (not (inReceptacle apple1 microwave))
        (not (inReceptacle apple2 microwave))
        (not (inReceptacle apple3 microwave))
        (not (inReceptacle apple1 pan))
        (not (inReceptacle apple2 pan))
        (not (inReceptacle apple3 pan))
        (atLocation apple1 table)
        (atLocation apple2 table)
        (atLocation apple3 table)
        (atLocation tomato table)
        (atLocation bread table)
        (atLocation shaker1 table)
        (atLocation shaker2 table)
        (atLocation spoon table)
        (atLocation microwave table) ; assuming microwave is accessible from table area for simplicity
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isHot apple1) ; or apple2 or apple3, any one apple heated
    ))
)
```

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )

final.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )
